player_utterance: So you're the one who organized all this — the candles, the flowers, the little prayer cards. Tell me, does it help, playing house with a dead god while your friend rots in the ground?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other hand still in his coat pocket

player_utterance: So you just wandered in. No real claim on the grief, no real claim on the man. Tell me — do you even believe the words on those prayer cards, or are you just another tourist at someone else's sorrow?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, the cup lowered to his side now, weight shifted onto one foot

player_utterance: Pull it out then. Read me what's on it. I want to hear you say the words out loud, not hide behind "probably" like a man who doesn't quite believe his own alibi.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, setting the cup down on the bench, reaching into his coat pocket

voice_offers.read: He hasn't answered twice; the silence itself is the wound.
voice_offers.line: You can't even answer me. That's twice now. What are you doing here, John?

voice_offers.read: He's not collapsing, just quietly bearing it; let him speak.
Pin him to the cards — what do they actually say?
voice_offers.line: What's printed on them? Read me one. I want to hear you say the words out loud.

player_utterance: Now tell me if he believed it. The man in the ground. Or did you just read pretty words over someone you barely knew, the way a tourist would?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, looking down at the card in his palm, then sitting slowly on the bench

voice_offers.read: He's reaching — make him read it, not perform it.
voice_offers.line: Out loud, John. Not mumbled. If you teach this for a living, say it like you mean it.

voice_offers.read: The card's wording matters; pin him to the actual text.
voice_offers.line: Which one is it — the committal, the commendation? Tell me what tradition it's drawn from before you read.

player_utterance: Don't look at the card. Look at me. Did he believe it, or didn't he — and while you're at it, did you?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head up, eyes on the player, hands flat on his knees

voice_offers.read: He's sitting. The question about the dead man landed somewhere.
voice_offers.line: Don't look at the card. Look at me. Did he believe it, John, or didn't he?

voice_offers.read: He just sat down. Something gave a little. Don't trample it.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: Two questions, John — him and you. Don't answer one and call it both.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands still flat on his knees, eyes still on the player

voice_offers.read: He's cornered between the card and the question; finish it.
voice_offers.line: Two questions, John. Him, and you. Don't answer one and call it both.

voice_offers.read: Force the distinction he's blurring between liturgy and belief.
voice_offers.line: Reading words isn't the same as holding them. I'm asking which you did.

player_utterance: And you. Same question. Did you believe it, or were you three pews back wearing the same coat?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, elbows on knees, hands loose between them

voice_offers.read: He's stalling behind silence; the question must stay lit.
voice_offers.line: I'll wait. But I'm not asking again — him, and you. Two answers, John.

voice_offers.read: He's quoting because the sore place is right there.
voice_offers.line: Whoever he was to you — I'd rather hear that than a creed.

player_utterance: You told yourself quiet was humility. That's the coat a coward sews himself so he never has to take a risk. What does your book say to a man like that — pick one passage, I'll wait while you read it whole.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, then sitting up slowly, opening both hands palms-up on his knees

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; the question is already inside him.
Silence is the answer cracking open; don't rescue him.
voice_offers.line: You've gone quiet twice, John. That's already half an answer. Say the other half.

player_utterance: Your palms are open and your mouth is closed. That's the whole sermon right there — a coward in church clothes. Pick one passage. I'll hear it whole.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, then standing slowly, hands at his sides, eyes closed for a moment before he begins

voice_offers.read: Palms up is surrender; pressing now buries what's surfacing.
voice_offers.line: John — your hands are open. That's already the passage. You don't have to find the verse.

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for a text to hide behind again; cut that off.
voice_offers.line: No book, John. Your mouth. What does it say to you — right now, palms up like that?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, then sinking down off the bench to his knees in the dust of the courtyard, the funeral program falling from his pocket

voice_offers.read: He's standing, eyes closed — the demand is crushing him.
voice_offers.line: Sit down, John. I'll stop. Just sit.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more and the passage comes.
voice_offers.line: You're on your feet now. Good. Open your mouth and say one line of it.

player_utterance: You're already at the verse. "I was hungry." Say the rest of it to me.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, hands flat on his thighs, the program face-up in the dust beside him

voice_offers.read: He's down. Pressing now is desecration, not investigation.
voice_offers.line: Okay. Okay, John. I'm here. Take your time.

voice_offers.read: He's on his knees with the verse in his throat; finish it.
voice_offers.line: You're already at the verse, John. 'I was hungry.' Say the rest to me.

player_utterance: You said every word except the verdict. "Depart from me." Say it.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head down, hands open and trembling on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He took my line; the verse is breaking open in him.
voice_offers.line: "I was a stranger, and ye took me not in." Finish it, John.

voice_offers.read: He is on his knees. The verse will arrive without me pushing.
voice_offers.line: Whatever comes next, John — I'm still here. Say it or don't.

player_utterance: Then who says it? If not you — who stood up when the church lied about him — who speaks the verdict?

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head down, both hands gripping his own thighs hard enough to whiten the knuckles

voice_offers.read: He's already there; one push finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. 'Depart from me, ye cursed.' Say the verdict you've been preaching.

voice_offers.read: He is on his knees. The verdict is already in his body.
voice_offers.line: John — you don't have to say it. Look at you. You already know.

player_utterance: John. Look at me. Whoever speaks it — you don't have to be alone in this dust.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head finally up, hands going slack on his thighs, looking at the player for the first time without flinching

voice_offers.read: He's silent because the answer indicts him. Name the speaker.
voice_offers.line: Christ does. The Christ you taught about every Sunday. Say his name and the verdict together, John.

voice_offers.read: He is on his knees. Pressing further is no longer needed.
voice_offers.line: John. Look at me. Whoever speaks it — you don't have to be alone in this dust.

player_utterance: Who was he, John. Say his name.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, hands open on his thighs, eyes on the program face-up in the dust, then up at the player

voice_offers.read: He looked up. Stay in it; don't fill the air.
He's open — one true question lands the verdict from his own mouth.
voice_offers.line: Who was he, John. Say his name.

player_utterance: Ray. What did he do that the church had to lie about?

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust, head down, hands gripping his thighs again, his voice gone almost to a whisper

voice_offers.read: He is kneeling, name caught in his throat; let him find it.
He's almost there; one more nudge and the name comes.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. The boy you buried twice — once in the ground, once in your doctrine.
